## reference · grafana dashboard best practices
* The USE method monitors hardware resources: Utilization, Saturation, and Errors.
* The RED method monitors services: Rate, Errors, and Duration.
* The Four Golden Signals monitor user-facing systems: Latency, Traffic, Errors, and Saturation.
* Alert on symptoms, not causes.
* Use template variables to prevent dashboard sprawl.
* Methodical dashboards should reflect an observability strategy.
* Hierarchical dashboards with drill-downs improve navigation.
* Dashboard design should reflect service hierarchies.
* Use expressive charts with meaningful color and normalized axes.
* Directed browsing cuts down on guessing.
* Version-controlled dashboard JSON is essential.
* Regularly review existing dashboards to reduce sprawl.
* Use scripting libraries to generate dashboards for consistency.
* No editing in the browser; use variables for view changes.
* Browsing for dashboards should be the exception, not the rule.
* Perform experimentation and testing in a separate Grafana instance.
* A dashboard should tell a story or answer a question.
* Dashboards should reduce cognitive load, not add to it.
* Have a monitoring strategy to govern dashboard creation and design.
Sources: https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/dashboards/build-dashboards/best-practices/
